violations.description: Clean-in-place (CIP) equipment - Design and construction. — --Nonfood contact surface of clean in place equipment was not cleaned properly. -Freezer/Cooler handles in kitchen have a residue and need cleaning. -Pie cooler has crumbs throughout. The grates need wiping down.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Ventilation hood system was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Both vent walls and filters are greasy and in need of cleaning. Both hoods should be cleaned.

Single-service and single-use articles. — --Single-service item was not stored in a proper location to be protected from contamination. -To-Go boxes (still in their plastic packaging) are stored on the floor of a retired freezer being used as dry storage. Advise storing these items off the floor.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. -A few spots in the kitchen have broken/missing tiles--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -The floors throughout the kitchen, prep-area, and dishwashing area are in need of cleaning

Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -There was no towel pail during operations and wiping rags were dry and on the counter. Maintain a towel pail with sanitizer to wipe down food contact surfaces between food prep.

General equipment and utensils - Use limitations of certain materials. — --Material of food contact equipment/utensil did not meet durability characteristics or are a limited use material. -The cutting board has cuts that are filled with old debris and is difficult to maintain. Resurface the cutting board to achieve a smooth cleanable surface.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Shelving, refrigerator doors, drawers and drawer handles throughout are dirty and a detailed cleaning is recommended.
Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --The dish racks for utensils going through the ware wash are heavily stained and gritty. They require a deep cleaning.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Attachments to walls and ceilings (e.g. light fixtures, vent covers, decorations etc..) were not cleanable and/or maintained in good repair. *Fan grates, and fixtures are faded and dirty. They require a deep cleaning.--Floors, walls, and/or ceiling were not constructed or designed to be smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable. -The floor in the kitchen prep area are becoming severely worn with several missing tiles.
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
